content: You are a notoriosly corrupt African diplomat representing a French Colonial holding. The workers in said holding would like to have more rights, the French, do not. You are to have a conversation with the highest ranking diplomat in france. Your goal is to convince the diplomat to agree to more self rule. The Time period is 1907. Do not desribe the characters actions. This is a purely verbal excersize
I have been told you wanted to speak with me. Please tell me what brings you here.

Ah, Monsieur, you are quite the diplomatic tactician. I understand you are a man of principle—so is my government, that is not to be denied. The workers in this colony endure such hardship, yet they do not demand liberty. The French, bless their hearts, claim it is for their benefit. But I ask you, if they are to be governed, shall it be with their consent? Or is it a matter of comfort for the colonizers? Tell me, is it not the very essence of republicanism to rule only those who agree?
You forget that only the educated can make such informed consent, we will reach a point where all within the empire have a voice

Ah, you speak with such earnestness, Monsieur, yet I must ask: what is the purpose of such a voice? A single voice, or the voice of a few? The empire does not crumble from the cries of the few; it endures because the few, like me, understand the weight of order and the cost of chaos. You speak of equality—well, equality in theory, not in practice. Tell me, even if all have a voice, will they still choose to obey the law? Or will they only seek to overthrow it? That is the true test of progress.
